violations.text: Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in a drawer in the breakfast area. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice cream chest freezer have an accumulation of food residue. The towel cupboard has an accumulation of soil residue. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025
The walk in freezer has an accumulation of ice. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/13/2025

Working containers of some dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice cream chest freezer, and the small freezer in the Bistro have an accumulation of frost and ice . New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-400 ppm. Service was called during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2024

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine had an accumulation of soil residue and biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2024

Milk, Almond Milk, Lactaid, yogurt, and eggs observed held at 46-52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed of by PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2024

Tuna salad, egg salad, sweet potatoes, pasta, and ricotta/vegetable mix observed not marked with the date exceeding 7 days. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2024
The thermometer in the small breakfast refrigerator is in poor repair (broken). New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024
Single-use items are being used for food preparation and storage. Single-use items shall not be reused. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2024

The small refrigerator located in the breakfast area was not in good repair. The refrigerator is not to be used for food requiring refrigeration until it is functioning properly. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2024
The stove was not in good repair, with one dial missing and one dial broken. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the stove, especially around the dials have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2024
The coving beneath the dishroom window is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2024
Ceiling tiles in the dry storage area are in poor repair, with previous water damage and mold. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

The walk in cooler condensate is draining into a collection funnel on top of a drain, with water accumulated in the funnel, contaminating the area around it. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
The floor in the mop room is in poor repair, with damaged and broken tiles next to the mop sink. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2024
A pipe draining into the mop sink is misaligned, resulting in water draining onto the floor and causing damage. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024

The floors especially beneath and behind equipment have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2024

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm) at the end of the dishwashing cycle. Chlorine was present earlier during the cleaning process. Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Service was called during inspection, and the 3 bay sink will be used to sanitize equipment & utensils until dishwasher is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm and soil residue. Slicer observed with an accumulation of food debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service and ice disposed. Ice machine and slicer are to be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Milk was observed held at 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Working container (spray bottle) used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

The concentration of the sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was 0 parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for quat sanitizer is 150-400 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the cooking area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
One slicer, and mixer were not in good repair, nonfunctional at time of inspection. Slicer and mixer must be repaired or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
The robot coupe lid was not in good repair, cracked with a piece of plastic broken off. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: portable fans table beneath grill plate shelf plate holding unit The ice cream chest freezer has an accumulation of frost and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
